message:
docs: add API doc comments to combinator headers and reconcile README test counts

- Add concise doc comments to all public API functions in combinator.h
  (join, race, select, timeout, first-ok, quorum, race-timeout,
  retry-timeout) and combinator2.h (retry, bracket, pipeline, bulkhead,
  rate-limit, map-reduce), describing purpose and return semantics.

- Update README repo-layout section: replace stale hardcoded test counts
  (161 files / 2,011 cases / 116 unit files / 11 vignettes) with
  current tree counts (254 tracked files, 198 C test programs, 146 unit
  files, 12 vignettes).

- Record issue bd-3uas (reconcile stale README test counts) as closed
  in .beads/issues.jsonl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3tx","title":"Honor BUILD_DIR in conformance/profile-parity artifact generation","description":"Conformance/profile-parity helper scripts partially hardcoded build paths, causing failures when invoking with alternate BUILD_DIR (especially under rch worker permission differences). Ensure run_conformance.sh forwards BUILD_DIR to make build and run_profile_parity.sh writes adapter artifact under selected BUILD_DIR.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayRiver","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:33.021827010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:35.858189947Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:35.858161123Z","close_reason":"Patched tools/ci/run_conformance.sh to pass BUILD_DIR to make build, and tools/ci/run_profile_parity.sh to write adapter artifact under selected BUILD_DIR.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3uas","title":"Reconcile stale README repo-layout test counts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:31:13.206097014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:32:13.645128959Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:32:13.644864930Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vt","title":"EPIC: Advanced Alpha Levers and Research Backlog (No Feature Loss)","description":"Background:
The plan includes advanced ideas (formal assurance ladder, lock-free alternatives, cache-oblivious layouts, etc.). These should be pursued via controlled spikes with rollback and proof artifacts.

Goal:
Run high-EV research tasks that can elevate performance/assurance without semantic compromise.

Constraints:
- one lever at a time
- explicit baseline/profile/isomorphism artifacts
- fallback path mandatory

Why this serves users:
- keeps project ambitious without destabilizing the core delivery path.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.632150816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850235463Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850215876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","optimization","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.1","title":"Pilot formal assurance ladder from golden outputs to bounded model checks to translation validation","description":"Background:
The plan calls for replacing missing compile-time guarantees with layered assurance artifacts.

Subtasks:
- Define staged assurance levels and entry/exit criteria.
- Pilot bounded model checks on selected lifecycle/state-machine components.
- Define lightweight translation-validation checks for critical transformations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Produces concrete recommendation with cost/benefit and integration path.
- Includes fallback plan if tooling cost outweighs value.
- Leaves behind reusable templates for future formal efforts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.410198918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:50:56.417210097Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:50:56.417190430Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["assurance","formal","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.10","title":"Prototype optional Rust-AST zero-drift extractor for transition and invariant artifacts","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.3B allows an optional zero-drift extractor path to reduce manual transcription drift from Rust semantics.

Subtasks:
- Build minimal tooling prototype (Rust AST parser under `tools/`) that emits transition X-macros and invariant schema fragments.
- Compare generated artifacts against manually curated baseline for one subsystem.
- Define promotion criteria, review requirements, and rollback plan if extractor proves brittle.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates measurable drift-reduction value on sampled subsystem.
- Generated artifacts pass parity review before any adoption.
- Clear go/no-go decision and risk note are recorded.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:49:24.000169440Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:49:24.000147309Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","research","spec","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
